records.chapter: Desire for opposites hinders success
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 403. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Whoever hankers after contraries gets no means of success.
records.arabicText: ۴۰۳. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَنْ أَوْمَأَ إِلَى مُتَفَاوِتٍ خَذَلَتْهُ الْحِيَلُ.

records.chapter: Amīr al-mu’minīn's caution regarding al-Mughīrah's worldly motives
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 405. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, heard ‘Ammār ibn Yāsir (may Allāh have mercy on him) conversing with al-Mughīrah ibn Shu‘bah and said:
records.arabicText: ۴۰۵. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام) لِعَمَّارِ بْنِ يَاسِرٍ (رحمه الله) وَقَدْ سَمِعَهُ يُرَاجِعُ الْمُغِيرَةَ بْنَ شُعْبَةَ كَلَاماً:

records.chapter: Amīr al-mu’minīn's caution regarding al-Mughīrah's worldly motives
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: Let him alone O’ ‘Ammār, for he has entered religion only to the extent of his deriving advantage of the world, and he has willfully involved himself in misgivings in order to adopt them as cover for his shortcomings.
records.arabicText: دَعْهُ يَا عَمَّارُ، فَإِنَّهُ لَمْ يَأْخُذْ مِنَ الدِّينِ إِلَّا مَا قَارَبَهُ الدُّنْيَا، وَعَلَى عَمدٍ لَبَّسَ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ لِيَجْعَلَ الشُّبُهَاتِ عَاذِراً لِسَقَطَاتِهِ.

records.chapter: The heart as the eye's manuscript
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 409. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: The heart is the book of the eye.
records.arabicText: ٤٠٩. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): الْقَلْبُ مُصْحَفُ الْبَصَرِ.

records.chapter: Warning against challenging divine authority with speech
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 411. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Do not try the sharpness of your tongue against Him Who gave you the power to speak, nor the eloquence of your speaking against Him Who set you on the right path.
records.arabicText: ٤١١. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): لَا تَجْعَلَنَّ ذَرَبَ لِسَانِكَ عَلَى مَنْ أَنْطَقَكَ، وَبَلاَغَةَ قَوْلِكَ عَلَى مَنْ سَدَّدَكَ.

records.chapter: Enduring grief like noble beings
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 414. In another tradition it is related that Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said to al-Ash‘ath ibn Qays by way of condolence on the death of his son:
records.arabicText: ٤١٤. وَفِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ أَنَّهُ (عليه السلام) قَالَ لِلْأَشْعَثِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ مُعَزِّياً (عَنِ ابْنٍ لَهُ)

records.chapter: Enduring grief like noble beings
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: Either endure like great people or else you will forget like animals.
records.arabicText: إِنْ صَبَرْتَ صَبْرَ الْأَكَارِمِ، وَإِلَّا سَلَوْتَ سُلُوَّ الْبَهَائِمِ.

records.chapter: Clemency as familial kinship
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 418. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Clemency is (like) a kinsfolk.
records.arabicText: ٤١٨. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): الْحِلْمُ عَشِيرَةٌ.

records.chapter: Wisdom distinguishing guidance from misguidance
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 421. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: It is enough if your wisdom distinguishes for you the ways of going astray from those of guidance.
records.arabicText: ٤٢١. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): كَفَاكَ مِنْ عَقْلِكَ مَا أَوْضَحَ لَكَ سَبِيلَ غَيِّكَ مِنْ رُشْدِكَ.

records.chapter: Forbearance and wisdom as moral virtues
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 424. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Forbearance is a curtain for covering, and wisdom is a sharp sword. Therefore, conceal the weaknesses in your conduct with forbearance and kill your desires with your wisdom.
records.arabicText: ٤٢٤. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): الْحِلْمُ غِطَاءٌ سَاتِرٌ، وَالْعَقْلُ حُسَامٌ قَاطِعٌ، فَاسْتُرْ خَلَلَ خُلُقِكَ بِحِلْمِكَ، وَقَاتِلْ هَوَاكَ بِعَقْلِكَ.

records.chapter: Complaining to believers versus unbelievers
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 427. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Whoever complains about a need to a believer, it is as though he has complained about it to Allāh; but whoever complains about it to an unbeliever it is as though he complained about Allāh.
records.arabicText: ٤٢٧. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَنْ شَكَا الْحَاجَةَ إِلَى مُؤْمِنٍ فَكَأَنَّمَا شَكَاهَا إِلَى اللهِ، وَمَنْ شَكَاهَا إلَى كَافِرٍ فَكَأَنَّمَا شَكَا اللهَ.

records.chapter: Futility of striving against fate for wealth
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 430. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: The worst in bargaining and the most unsuccessful in striving is the man who exerts himself in seeking riches although fate does not help him in his aims and consequently he goes from this world in a sorrowful state while in the next world too he will face its ill consequences. [1]
records.arabicText: ٤٣٠. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): إِنَّ أَخْسَرَ النَّاسِ صَفْقَةً وَأَخْيَبَهُمْ سَعْياً، رَجُلٌ أَخْلَقَ بَدَنَهُ فِي طَلَبِ آمَالِهِ، وَلَمْ تُسَاعِدْهُ الْمَقَادِيرُ عَلَى إِرَادَتِهِ، فَخَرَجَ مِنَ الدُّنْيَا بِحَسْرَتِهِ، وَقَدِمَ عَلَى الْآخِرَةِ بِتَبِعَتِهِ.

records.chapter: True believers' inward devotion and detachment from worldly pursuits
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 432. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: The lovers of Allāh are those who look at the inward side of the world while the other people look at its outward side, they busy themselves with its remoter benefits while the other people busy themselves in the immediate benefits. They kill those things which they feared would have killed them, and they leave here in this world what they think would leave them. They took the amassing of wealth by others as a small matter and regarded it like losing. They are enemies of those things which others love while they love things which others hate. Through them, the Qur’ān has been learnt and they have been given knowledge through the Qur’ān. With them the Qur’ān is staying while they stand by the Qur’ān. They do not see any object of hope above what they hope and no object of fear above what they fear.
records.arabicText: ٤٣٢. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): إِنَّ أَوْلِيَاءَ اللهِ هُمُ الَّذِينَ نَظَرُوا إِلَى بَاطِنِ الدُّنْيَا إِذَا نَظَرَ النَّاسُ إِلَى ظَاهِرِهَا، وَاشْتَغَلُوا بِآجِلِهَا إِذَا اشْتَغَلَ النَّاسُ بِعَاجِلِهَا، فَأَمَاتُوا مِنْهَا مَا خَشُوا أَنْ يُمِيتَهُمْ وَتَرَكُوا مِنْهَا مَا عَلِمُوا أَنَّهُ سَيَتْرُكُهُمْ، وَرَأَوُا اسْتِكْثَارَ غَيْرِهِمْ مِنْهَا اسْتِقْلَالًا، وَدَرَكَهُمْ لَهَا فَوْتاً، أَعْدَاءُ مَا سَالَمَ النَّاسُ، وَسِلْمُ مَا عَادَى النَّاسُ! بِهِمْ عُلِمَ الْكِتَابُ وَبِهِ عُلِمُوا وَبِهِمْ قَامَ الْكِتَابُ وَبِهِ قَامُوا، لَا يَرَوْنَ مَرْجُوّاً فَوْقَ مَا يَرْجُونَ، وَلَا مَخُوفاً فَوْقَ مَا يَخَافُونَ.

records.chapter: Divine reciprocity in gratitude, prayer, and repentance
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 435. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: It is not that Allāh, to Whom belongs Might and Majesty, may keep the door of gratitude open for a person and close the door of plenty upon him, or to open the door of prayer to a person and close the door of acceptance upon him, or to open the door of repentance on a person and close the door of forgiveness upon him.
records.arabicText: ٤٣٥. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَا كَانَ اللهُ لِيَفْتَحَ عَلَى عَبْدٍ بَابَ الشُّكْرِ وَيُغْلِقَ عَنْهُ بَابَ الزِّيَادَةِ، وَلَا لِيَفْتَحَ عَلَى عَبْدٍ بَابَ الدُّعَاءِ وَيُغْلِقَ عَنْهُ بَابَ الْإِجَابَةِ، وَلَا لِيَفْتَحَ لِعَبْدٍ بَابَ التَّوْبَةِ وَيُغْلِقَ عَنْهُ بَابَ الْمَغْفِرَةِ.

records.chapter: People oppose the unknown
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 438. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: People are enemies of what they do not know.
records.arabicText: ٤٣٨. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): النَّاسُ أَعْدَاءُ مَا جَهِلُوا.

records.chapter: Leadership as the test of character
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 441. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Governing power is the proving ground for people.
records.arabicText: ٤٤١. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): الْوِلَايَاتُ مَضَامِيرُ الرِّجَالِ.

records.chapter: Enduring little surpasses fleeting abundance
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 444. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: A little that lasts is better than much that brings grief.
records.arabicText: ٤٤٤. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): قَلِيلٌ مَدُومٌ عَلَيْهِ خَيْرٌ مِنْ كَثِيرٍ مَمْلُولٍ مِنْهُ.

records.chapter: Ignorance of religious law leads to usury
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 447. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Whoever trades without knowing the rules of religious law will be involved in usury.
records.arabicText: ٤٤٧. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَنِ اتَّجَرَ بِغَيْرِ فِقْهٍ فَقَدِ ارْتَطَمَ فِي الرِّبَا.

records.chapter: Diminishing wit through excessive joking
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 450. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Whenever a man cuts a joke he separates away a bit from his wit.
records.arabicText: ۴۵۰. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَا مَزَحَ امْرُؤٌ مَزْحَةً إِلَّا مَجَّ مِنْ عَقْلِهِ مَجَّةً.

records.chapter: Amīr al-mu’minīn on az-Zubayr's allegiance and his son's betrayal
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 453. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: az-Zubayr remained a man from our house till his wretched son ‘Abdullāh came forth. [1]
records.arabicText: ٤٥٣. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَا زَالَ الزُّبَيْرُ رَجُلاً مِنَّا أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ حَتَّى نَشَأَ ابْنُهُ الْمَشْؤُومُ عَبْدُ اللهِ.

records.chapter: Amīr al-mu’minīn's view on the greatest poet
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 455. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, was asked about the greatest poet and he said:
records.arabicText: ٤٥٥. وَسُئِلَ (عليه السلام) : مَنْ أَشْعَرُ الشُّعَرَاءِ؟ فقال (عليه السلام) :

records.chapter: Amīr al-mu’minīn's view on the greatest poet
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: The whole group of them did not proceed on the same lines in such a way that we can know the height of their glory; but if it has to be done then it is the “al-Mālik aḍ-ḍillīl” (the mislead king).
records.arabicText: إِنَّ الْقَوْمَ لَمْ يَجْرُوا فِي حَلبةٍ تُعْرَفُ الْغَايَةُ عِنْدَ قَصَبَتِهَا فَإِنْ كَانَ وَلَا بُدَّ فَالْمَلِكُ الضِّلِّيلُ.

records.chapter: Amīr al-mu’minīn's view on the greatest poet
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: as-Sayyid ar-Raḍī says : Amīr al-mu’minīn means Imriu’l-Qays. [1]
records.arabicText: قال السيد الرضي: يريد امرأ القيس

records.chapter: Preferring truth over falsehood despite consequences
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 458. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Belief means that you should prefer truth (even) when it harms you rather than falsehood (even) when it benefits you; that your words should not be more than your action and that you should fear Allāh when speaking about others.
records.arabicText: ٤٥٨. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): (عَلَامَةُ) الْإِيمَانُ أَنْ تُؤثِرَ الصِّدْقَ حَيْثُ يَضُرُّكَ عَلَى الْكَذِبِ حَيْثُ يَنْفَعُكَ، وَأَلَّا يَكُونَ فِي حَديِثِكَ فَضْلٌ عَنْ عِلْمِكَ وَأَنْ تَتَّقِيَ اللهَ فِي حَدِيثِ غَيْرِكَ.

records.chapter: Backbiting as the weapon of the powerless
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 461. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Backbiting is the tool of the helpless.
records.arabicText: ٤٦١. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): الْغِيبَةُ جُهْدُ الْعَاجزِ.
